title: Coherence dynamics in quantum many-body systems with conservation laws
authors: Sreemayee Aditya, Emanuele Tirrito, Piotr Sierant, Xhek Turkeshi
abstract: We study how conservation laws shape the spreading of quantum coherence in many-body dynamics. Focusing on $U(1)$-symmetric random circuits, charge-and-dipole conserving circuits, as well as ergodic Hamiltonian dynamics, we probe coherences both globally, via the participation entropy, and locally, via the relative entropy of coherence. Combining exact vector evolution, matrix product state simulations, and replica tensor networks methods, we find that conservation laws replace the logarithmic saturation of unconstrained circuits with slow hydrodynamic relaxation of the global coherence measures. Locally, symmetry-constrained circuits show a clean rise-peak-fall structure whose peak time grows algebraically with subsystem size. In contrast, ergodic Hamiltonians broaden the peak into an extended plateau at larger subsystems, highlighting a qualitatively distinct mechanism. Coherence thus emerges as a sensitive probe of symmetry-constrained thermalization, linking quantum resource dynamics to many-body transport.
summary: This paper explores how conservation laws, such as charge and dipole moments, fundamentally alter the way quantum coherence spreads in many-body systems. Unlike unconstrained random circuits that saturate logarithmically, symmetric systems exhibit slow, power-law hydrodynamic relaxation. The authors demonstrate that local coherence follows a distinct rise-peak-fall pattern, and they provide a unified analytical framework to calculate the asymptotic saturation values of entanglement and purity in these symmetry-constrained sectors.
main_problem: Investigating how conservation laws (symmetries) shape the spreading of quantum coherence and the relaxation dynamics in many-body systems.
main_result: Conservation laws replace the logarithmic saturation of unconstrained circuits with slow hydrodynamic relaxation, where global coherence decays via power-law and late-time exponential regimes, and local coherence exhibits a characteristic rise-peak-fall structure.
method: The study combines exact state-vector evolution, Matrix Product State (MPS) simulations, and Replica Tensor Network (RTQ) methods with analytical approaches like Haar averaging and mapping to stochastic models (SSEP).
model_or_system: The paper analyzes U(1)-symmetric random circuits (spin-1/2 and spin-1), charge-and-dipole-conserving (fractonic) circuits, and ergodic Hamiltonian dynamics via the mixed-field Ising model.
key_observables: Global participation entropy (S2), local relative entropy of coherence (Cd), Renyi-2 entanglement entropy (SR), and subsystem purity.
important_parameters: System size (L), subsystem size (LA), conservation laws (U(1) charge and dipole), and the distinction between Z-basis and tilted initial states.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes open boundary conditions and that the dynamics are restricted to specific symmetry sectors or Krylov fragments.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates global and local coherence spreading, showing the saturation of global entropy and the rise-peak-fall profile of local coherence. Figure 4 shows the dynamics of Sd, SR, and Cd for spin-1/2 chains. Figure 14 depicts the tensor-network construction for computing diagonal purity.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of coherence spreading under symmetries, presents numerical results for U(1) and fractonic circuits, provides analytical derivations for Haar-averaged saturation values, and concludes with a stochastic mapping to explain the relaxation mechanisms.
why_interesting: It provides a deep link between quantum resource dynamics (coherence) and many-body transport/hydrodynamics, showing how symmetries act as a bottleneck for thermalization.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Global and local spreading of quantum coherence. A product incoherent state on L qubits is evolved by a brick-wall circuit of gates U(∆τ), realizing U(1)-symmetric or charge-and-dipole conserving random dynamics, or under the mixed-field Ising Hamiltonian. Red shading marks the region over which the global/ local resource is maximum, while the gray region indicates minimal coherence resource. (a) Global dynamics: Sd saturates on the full chain at a time t sat c ∝Lα. (b) Local dynamics on a subsystem A of size LA: for symmetric circuits Cd(A, t) peaks at τ m c ∝Lαm A before relaxing to a free state, while there exists a peak-to-plateau crossover with LA in Hamiltonian evolution.
FIG. 2. Global Sd dynamics of a spin- 1

FIG. 3. Global Sd dynamics of a spin-1, U(1)-conserving circuit with L sites: (a) Sd grows rapidly and saturates to the value set by the U(1) symmetry. (b) At late times, ∆Sd exhibits exponential decay, ∆Sd = Ape−t/τp, with τp ∝L1.766
FIG. 4. Local coherence dynamics in a U(1)-conserving spin- 1
FIG. 5. Local ∆Sd decay in a U(1)-conserving spin- 1

FIG. 6. Local coherence dynamics in a U(1)-conserving spin-1 circuit with L sites (RTN): (a–c) Dynamics of local Sd, SR, and Cd for different subsystem sizes LA at L = 72. (d) The intermediate-time decay of Cd exhibits power-law tails along with the time scale at which Cd attains its maximum scales as a power law with subsystem size, τ m c ∝Lαm A , with αm ≃0.40 (see inset). Results correspond to a period-3 spin-1 N´eel initial state; RTN simulations are performed with bond dimension χ = 640.

FIG. 7. Local ∆Sd decay in a U(1)-conserving spin-1 circuit with L sites for a subsystem of size LA: (a-b) The local ∆Sd similar to the spin-1/2 case exhibits a dynamical crossover from intermediate-time power-law decay, ∼t−βSd with βSd ≃1.08, to late-time exponential decay, ∼ASde−t/τSd . The crossover regime is highlighted by magenta squares. (c) τcr exhibits power-law scaling with L with exponent 1.126. (d) The late-time exponential decay-time scale grows, τSd ∝L1.721. Results are obtained for a period-3 N´eel initial state. Data for L = 12–15 are computed using exact state-vector simulations averaged over at least 4000 circuit realizations, while results for L ≥21 are obtained using RTN...
FIG. 8. Global Sd dynamics in a spin- 1

FIG. 9. Local coherence dynamics in the spin-1/2 charge- and dipole-conserving circuit with L sites. (a–c) Dynamics of the local Sd, SR, and Cd for different subsystem sizes LA at L = 48. (d) The peak time of Cd scales as τ m c ∝Lαm A with αm ≃0.313. Results use the same initial state as in Fig. 8 and are obtained from exact state-vector simulations averaged over at least 104 circuit realizations.

FIG. 10. Local ∆Sd decay in charge- and dipole-conserving circuits with L sites and subsystem size LA. (a,b) The relaxation exhibits an intermediate power-law regime ∆Sd(t) ∼t−βSd with βSd ≃1.548, crossing over to a late-time exponential decay ASd e−t/τSd with τSd ∝L1.893 (panel d). In panels (a,b), magenta squares mark the crossover between the two regimes; the crossover time scales as τcr ∝L1.048 (panel c). Results use the same initial state as in Fig. 8 and are obtained from exact state-vector simulations averaged over at least 104 circuit realizations for L = 24 −48 and LA = 4.
